channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: VECRA issues Emergency Action Notice to multi-storey early learning service over rooftop safety risks
item.description: The Victorian Early Childhood Regulatory Authority (VECRA) has issued an Emergency Action Notice (EAN) to the approved provider of Woodlands St Kilda Road after identifying serious risks to children's safety associated with a rooftop outdoor play area and emergency evacuation planning.
item.content:encoded: The Victorian Early Childhood Regulatory Authority (VECRA) has issued an Emergency Action Notice (EAN) to the approved provider of Woodlands St Kilda Road after identifying serious risks to children's safety associated with a rooftop outdoor play area and emergency evacuation planning.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Queensland Health updates ‘Time Out’ resources for contagious conditions
item.description: Queensland Health has updated its Time Out resources, providing refreshed guidance on exclusion periods for children and staff with contagious conditions in early childhood education and care services and schools.
item.content:encoded: Queensland Health has updated its Time Out resources, providing refreshed guidance on exclusion periods for children and staff with contagious conditions in early childhood education and care services and schools.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Children’s Week grants open across Australia to support child-centred community events
item.description: Children’s Week 2026 will see organisations across Australia deliver events celebrating children’s rights, culture, identity and participation, supported by grant programs operating in Queensland, Victoria, South Australia, Western Australia and the Australian Capital Territory.
item.content:encoded: Children’s Week 2026 will see organisations across Australia deliver events celebrating children’s rights, culture, identity and participation, supported by grant programs operating in Queensland, Victoria, South Australia, Western Australia and the Australian Capital Territory.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: New ECU tool helps teachers see school through children’s eyes
item.description: Researchers from Edith Cowan University’s (ECU’s) School of Education have developed a practical guide to understanding and monitoring young children’s learner engagement.
item.content:encoded: Researchers from Edith Cowan University’s (ECU’s) School of Education have developed a practical guide to understanding and monitoring young children’s learner engagement.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: FDC Construction closes in on $950m-plus ASX float - The Australian
item.description: FDC Construction closes in on $950m-plus ASX float - The Australian
item.content:encoded: FDC Construction closes in on $950m-plus ASX float - The Australian
item.dc:creator: The Australian

item.title: What US childcare centre closures could mean for Australia's ECEC sector
item.description: The quiet closure of around 90 childcare centres by a major US provider Bright Horizons over the past three years has prompted fresh discussion about the sustainability of early learning markets globally.
item.content:encoded: The quiet closure of around 90 childcare centres by a major US provider Bright Horizons over the past three years has prompted fresh discussion about the sustainability of early learning markets globally.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Childcare workers have been guaranteed a pay bump. What’s the fine print?
item.description: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Investing in educators means investing in children
item.description: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Extension of early childhood worker retention payment welcome amid ongoing reform efforts - thepoint.com.au
item.description: Extension of early childhood worker retention payment welcome amid ongoing reform efforts - thepoint.com.au
item.content:encoded: Extension of early childhood worker retention payment welcome amid ongoing reform efforts - thepoint.com.au
item.dc:creator: thepoint.com.au

item.title: Government to extend pay subsidy to avoid childcare worker walk-off - Australian Broadcasting Corporation
item.description: Government to extend pay subsidy to avoid childcare worker walk-off - Australian Broadcasting Corporation
item.content:encoded: Government to extend pay subsidy to avoid childcare worker walk-off - Australian Broadcasting Corporation
item.dc:creator: Australian Broadcasting Corporation

item.title: Keeping children's data safe: How Xplor education is raising the bar on technology security
item.description: As cyber threats to the education sector grow more sophisticated, Xplor‘s Chief Security Officer Kevin Kanji explains why we consider robust technology safety as a fundamental obligation to families and educators alike.
item.content:encoded: As cyber threats to the education sector grow more sophisticated, Xplor‘s Chief Security Officer Kevin Kanji explains why we consider robust technology safety as a fundamental obligation to families and educators alike.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Government commits $3.6 billion to permanently extend early childhood educator wage support
item.description: The Australian Government has committed $3.6 billion to permanently extend the Worker Retention Payment, securing the landmark 15 per cent wage increase for early childhood education and care (ECEC) educators beyond its original funding period.
item.content:encoded: The Australian Government has committed $3.6 billion to permanently extend the Worker Retention Payment, securing the landmark 15 per cent wage increase for early childhood education and care (ECEC) educators beyond its original funding period.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: SNAICC Newsletter – May 2026
item.description: The post SNAICC Newsletter – May 2026 appeared first on SNAICC – National Voice for our Children .
item.content:encoded: The post SNAICC Newsletter – May 2026 appeared first on SNAICC – National Voice for our Children .
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Elevating child safety & educator well being
item.description: The early childhood education sector is trusted with the care of young children every day, making child safety, compliance and educator wellbeing essential.
item.content:encoded: The early childhood education sector is trusted with the care of young children every day, making child safety, compliance and educator wellbeing essential.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Toy Story 5 pits traditional toys vs a tablet. In real life, families can combine the two
item.description: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.content:encoded: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Report shows how young people want to use their voice for change
item.description: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.content:encoded: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples ready to work in partnership for a safer future for Queensland children
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.

item.title: From Country to Classroom: SNAICC Launches Culturally Grounded Learning Tools
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC
